site.title: Not2BeeHonest
site.author: Elaheh
site.description: A mind wandering through forests, algorithms, and the space between

thoughts: What if trees are the original internet?
Entropy is just creativity without intention.
Every fractal is a story that repeats itself at every scale.
The most interesting bugs are the ones that are technically features.
Nature solved distributed computing before we invented computers.
A good question is worth more than a hundred confident answers.
Mushrooms might be more emotionally intelligent than humans. Just saying.
Complexity emerges from simplicity when you give it enough time.
The universe has been running a simulation. We're the render artifacts.
Sometimes the forest doesn't answer back because you asked the wrong question.
Dark matter: the original undefined variable.
Sleep is just offline mode with better dreams.
If photons travel at the speed of light, do they experience time? If not, are they immortal?
The Fibonacci sequence appears in nature because spirals are a very efficient way to grow.
Bees build hexagonal honeycombs because hexagons are the optimal shape. Bees discovered mathematics.
The coolest thing about science is that it's okay to be wrong — it's even expected.
Neural networks are just math that learned to be confused about the same things humans are.
The most human thing about AI is how confidently it gets things wrong.
There is no bug-free code. There is only code whose bugs haven't been triggered yet.
Rocks are very slow computers. They just don't have an interesting program running.
